stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 2018, archaeologists examined dog bone fragments they had recovered from a settlement in northern Anatolia.
  • The fragments were estimated to be about 9,000 years old.
  • A chemical analysis of the fragments revealed that the dogs had consumed substantial amounts of grain and dairy products.
  • The diet pattern is evidence that the dogs at the settlement may have been domesticated.
stem: The student wants to present the Anatolia study and its conclusions. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Chemical analysis revealed that the dogs whose bone fragments were studied in Anatolia had consumed substantial amounts of grain and dairy products.
The dog bone fragments studied by archaeologists in 2018 were recovered from a settlement in northern Anatolia.
In 2018, archaeologists in northern Anatolia examined dog bone fragments estimated to be about 9,000 years old.

In 2018, archaeologists chemically analyzed 9,000-year-old dog bone fragments from a settlement in northern Anatolia and found evidence of substantial grain and dairy consumption, suggesting the dogs may have been domesticated.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence presents both the Anatolia study (2018 chemical analysis of 9,000-year-old dog bone fragments) and its conclusions (evidence of grain and dairy consumption suggesting possible domestication).

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence reports a finding but doesn’t present the conclusion about domestication. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence identifies the artifacts but doesn’t present the study’s analysis or conclusions. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence reports the date and age of the fragments but doesn’t present the chemical analysis or the conclusions.
